messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I GOT THE JOB.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: WAIT. The one at the design firm?!

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: YES. They just called me like two minutes ago. I'm literally shaking.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: AMY! Oh my god, that's amazing! You crushed that interview, I knew it.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I didn't know it! I thought I totally bombed the portfolio review.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Are you kidding? Your portfolio is insane. They'd have been stupid not to hire you.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I start in two weeks. Two weeks, Bob!

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: We're celebrating tonight. I'm picking the restaurant and you're not allowed to say no.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I wasn't going to! Get somewhere with champagne.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Obviously. I'm so proud of you, seriously.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I keep rereading the offer letter to make sure it's real.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Hey Bob, have you tried that new coffee place on Main Street?

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: No, not yet! Is it any good?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: So good. I had their oat milk latte and it honestly changed my life.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: That's a bold claim. I'm more of a black coffee person though.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: They do a really solid pour-over too, if that's your thing.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Okay, you've convinced me. Want to go tomorrow morning?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Yes! Let's do like 9ish?

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Perfect, I'll meet you there.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I think I'm going to quit my job.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Wait, really? What happened?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Nothing dramatic. I just feel like I've been on autopilot for months.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Yeah, I get that. Have you thought about what you'd do next?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Maybe go back to school. Maybe travel for a bit. I don't know yet.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: That's kind of exciting though, right? Having a blank slate.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Terrifying is the word I'd use.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: A little of both, probably. Whatever you decide, I've got your back.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Thanks, Bob. That actually means a lot.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Hey. Are you around?

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Yeah, what's going on?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I got let go today.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: What? Are you serious?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Yeah. They called me into HR at like 3pm. Said they were "restructuring."

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: That's such garbage. You were one of the hardest workers there.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Apparently that doesn't matter when they need to cut budget.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: How are you doing?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I don't really know yet. It hasn't fully hit me. I just drove around for an hour before coming home.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Do you need anything?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I think I just needed to tell someone.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: I'm glad you called me. We'll figure this out, okay?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Yeah. Okay.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Hey! How was your weekend?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: It was fine, I guess.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: That doesn't sound very convincing. You okay?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Yeah, I don't know. My dog's been really sick and the vet isn't sure what's wrong.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Oh no, Amy. I'm so sorry. How long has this been going on?

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: About a week now. He's just not himself. Won't eat, barely moves.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: That's heartbreaking. He's such a sweet dog.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: I just keep thinking about the worst case scenario, you know?

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: I know, but try not to go there yet. Vets figure things out all the time.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Yeah. I just hate seeing him like that.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Do you want some company tonight? I can bring dinner over and we can hang out with him.

messages.speaker: Amy
messages.text: Honestly, that would be really nice. Thank you.

messages.speaker: Bob
messages.text: Of course. I'll be there around six.
